meta_title: For Founders: marketing automation to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to marketing automation and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Marketing Automation to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop searching for ideas. Use this beginner-friendly checklist to build marketing automation SaaS tools using Antigravity and Claude Code to drive revenue.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing 'perfect' ideas. The most profitable SaaS products are boring automation tools that solve manual marketing bottlenecks. By building micro-SaaS marketing scrapers or content schedulers, you solve real pain points. Use Antigravity for rapid prototyping and Claude Code for autonomous feature implementation to launch your MVP in days, not months.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You’ve spent weeks scrolling through forums, paralyzed by the fear of building something nobody wants. Meanwhile, marketing teams are drowning in manual, repetitive tasks—data entry, content repurposing, and lead qualification—that are begging for a software solution.

The secret isn't a 'big idea'; it's a 'small automation.' By focusing on high-friction marketing tasks, you move from an aimless builder to a problem-solver with a clear path to monetization. Let’s strip away the noise and build your first revenue-generating engine using the power of modern AI development tools.

steps.title: Identify the High-Friction Marketing Loop
steps.description: Find a task that takes a marketer 2+ hours daily. We will automate the data ingestion phase of a lead-gen workflow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze common marketing tasks on LinkedIn and Twitter. Suggest 3 micro-SaaS ideas that automate data extraction and lead qualification with low technical overhead.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Research top 5 manual marketing tasks and output a feasibility report for a micro-SaaS MVP.'

steps.title: Bootstrap the MVP Architecture
steps.description: Define the core data flow. We need a clean stack that handles API inputs and outputs without unnecessary bloat.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a serverless architecture using Next.js and Supabase for a marketing automation tool that scrapes target audience data and stores it in a structured database.

steps.title: Implement the Core Automation Logic
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write the actual automation logic that connects your data source to your processing engine.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust function that monitors a specific marketing channel API, filters for high-intent keywords, and formats the output into a clean JSON object for CRM integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement logic to fetch and filter marketing channel API responses using a secure environment variable setup.'

steps.title: Build the User-Facing Dashboard
steps.description: Create a simple interface where users can toggle their automation settings and view their processed data logs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, minimalist UI component in React that displays automation run history and allows users to trigger a manual refresh.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a dashboard component showing real-time automation statistics and a manual refresh trigger button.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate with Real Data
steps.description: Push your code to production and perform a live test to ensure the automation loop functions without human intervention.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a checklist for production deployment, including essential error logging, rate-limit management, and database indexing for high performance.

pro_tips: Focus on 'Read-Only' automations first; scraping data is safer and faster to build than tools that post content.
Always build an 'Export to CSV' button; it's the fastest way to add value without building complex CRM integrations.
Use rate-limiting as a feature; tell users your tool is 'smart' because it respects platform API limits to keep their accounts safe.
Don't build auth until you have your first 10 beta testers; use magic links or simple API keys to save weeks of dev time.
Monitor your own logs; the errors your users encounter are the features they will pay you to fix.
resources: Indie Hackers Marketing Automation Forum: https://indiehackers.com
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, find 5 users on Twitter or LinkedIn who are manually performing the task you just automated. Offer them free access in exchange for a testimonial and feedback on your pricing model.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the blueprint. The only thing missing is your execution. Don't let another day pass building 'hobby' projects that go nowhere. Solve a real problem, charge for it, and scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
